description_long: This is a weathered wooden picket fence section, featuring vertical slats with pointed tops and a distressed green paint finish. The slats are unevenly spaced and some are slightly misaligned, giving the fence a rustic, aged appearance. Two horizontal rails run across the back, providing structural support and connecting the pickets. The wood shows signs of wear, with visible texture and discoloration from exposure to the elements. There are no decorative elements or post caps, and the construction is simple and utilitarian. This type of fence is commonly found in residential yards, gardens, or parks, serving as a boundary or barrier.
description: A rustic, weathered green wooden picket fence with pointed tops and two horizontal support rails. The slats are unevenly spaced and show signs of age and outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the fence, with pointed pickets, uneven spacing, and visible green paint wear.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the alignment of the vertical slats.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the two horizontal support rails and the backs of the pickets.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the staggered arrangement of the slats.
